math_recheck: Re-derived & numerically verified: HK reductio (strict</=) matches Nobel lecture; T=T_s+T_c and F=T_s+U_H+E_xc match Capelle (53)-(55); total energy E=Sum eps - U_H - int v_xc n + E_xc matches Capelle (73) AND equals the direct functional to 1e-14 (band-sum=direct=1.65613/19.92793 Ha confirmed by running the saved solver); LDA exchange E_x=-(3/4)(3/pi)^(1/3) int n^(4/3), v_x=-(3/pi)^(1/3)n^(1/3), and the v_x^LDA=(2/3)*Slater(alpha=1) ratio all check (2/3 exact); adiabatic-connection E_xc=1/2 int n n_bar_xc/|r-r'| and sum rule int n_xc=-1 match Argaman-Makov (28)-(29); e_x=-0.458/r_s matches Nobel (5.3); C_F=2.871.
fixes: answer.md:73 softened anachronistic 'high-accuracy quantum Monte Carlo' to 'improvable to higher precision' to keep the distilled file in-frame (QMC for the e-gas postdates the work)
caveat: Original 1964 Hohenberg-Kohn (Phys.Rev.136,B864) and 1965 Kohn-Sham (Phys.Rev.140,A1133) papers could NOT be obtained as free full text (APS+Cloudflare; the refs/src 'hk-*.pdf'/'ks-*.pdf' files are HTML landing pages, not PDFs). Every load-bearing equation was instead verified against Kohn's 1999 Nobel lecture (real text) + Capelle (cond-mat/0211443) + Argaman-Makov (physics/9806013), all retrieved and cross-checked. Gap is recorded in notes/synthesis.md.
